title: The High Court at Bombay (Extension of Jurisdiction to Goa, Daman and Diu) Act, 1981
full_text: The High Court at Bombay (Extension of Jurisdiction to Goa, Daman and Diu) Act, 1981 मुम्‍बई उच्‍च न्‍यायालय (गोवा, दमण और दीव पर अधिकारिता का विस्‍तारण) अधिनियम, 1981 Rules Regulations Notifications Orders Circulars Ordinance Statutes SectionsScheduleAnnexureAppendixFormsActdetails Act ID: 198126 Act Number: 26 Enactment Date: 1981-09-09 Act Year: 1981 Short Title: The High Court at Bombay (Extension of Jurisdiction to Goa, Daman and Diu) Act, 1981 Hindi Title: मुम्‍बई उच्‍च न्‍यायालय (गोवा, दमण और दीव पर अधिकारिता का विस्‍तारण) अधिनियम, 1981 Long Title: An Act to provide for the extension of the jurisdiction of the High Court at Bombay to the Union territory of Goa, Daman and Diu, for the establishment of a permanent bench of that High Court at Panaji and for matters connected therewith. Ministry: Ministry of Law and Justice Department: Department of Justice Enforcement Date: 30-10-1982 Notification: 30th October, 1982, vide notification No. G.S.R. 592(E), dated 8th October, 1982, see Gazette of India, Extraordinary, Part II, sec. 3(i). TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles TitleFiles The High Court at Bombay (Extension of Jurisdiction to Goa, Daman and Diu) Act, 1981 Show all section Section 1. Short title and commencement. Show Related Subordinates Section 2. Definitions. Section 3. Extension of jurisdiction of Bombay High Court to Goa, Daman and Diu. Section 4. Jurisdiction of Bombay High Court. Section 5. Chapter VI of Part VI of the Constitution to apply to the Bombay High Court exercising jurisdiction over the Union territory of Goa, Daman and Diu. Section 6. Special provisions relating to advocates. Section 7. Transfer of pending proceedings from the Court of the Judicial Commissioner to the Bombay High Court. Section 8. Right to appear or act in proceedings transferred to the Bombay High Court. Section 9. Establishment of a permanent bench of Bombay High Court at Panaji. Section 10. Allocation of expenditure of the Bombay High Court. Section 11. Rule of construction. Section 12. Repealed. Section 13. Power to remove difficulties. Section 14. Power to adapt laws.
